In a nutshell, the MDG product versions are:

  • SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition, master data governance (with the same scope as the on-premise version SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA)
  • SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition, master data governance
  • SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition

For more details on versions, scope and deployment options, see the graphic below.

Fig.: Overview of SAP Master Data Governance versions in the context of centrally-managed master data.

See also the SAP Community page on MDG cloud deployments.

Now there is a much more easy-to-consume and interactive way for MDG beginners to get a quick understanding of the MDG product versions: Feel free to start the learning journey on learning@sap.com (LSC) and explore the SAP Master Data Governance deployments. It is free-of charge, doesn't take long, and you can even make it at your own chosen speed. 

I wish you a happy journey! And to stay on track with SAP Master Data Governance after having completed the learning journey, simply follow the SAP Community Topic Page for SAP Master Data Governance, read the  MDG-related blogs, and join the  Q&A forum.

Markus

entry.title: Analyze License Consumption Consolidated Data with the SAM4U Solution from SAP
entry.content:
Are you still paying for license analysis consultancy services that use their proprietary tools, marketed as miracle solutions? Forget about it! Now you can leverage an SAP solution that offers beautifully designed Fiori views to analyze user, engine, and even HANA Enterprise Edition license consumption.

Introducing SAM4U: The SAP Solution for License Analysis

Meet SAM4U, the innovative solution designed by SAP to streamline your license analysis. You can explore the demo version right from our website.

Today’s post is about consolidated data analysis and how you can create a consolidation corresponding to your measurement plan in just a few minutes.

When you install the SAM4U extractor to your backend system (read more about it in our previous blogs), you can start piping data to SAM4U, for instance, via a ZIPped file.

Pic. 01. Uploading ZIP File Produced by SAM4U Extractor

If you're uploading a file for the first time, it will also create system metadata from the extracted dataset. Each subsequent upload is grouped at the system level, allowing you to drill down and see all datasets you’ve uploaded:

 Pic. 02. Datasets Overview for a Certain System

Once you’ve uploaded data for all systems for your consolidation, navigate to the “Consolidation” view. Click the create button to join all the systems you’d like to consolidate. You can even add HANA EE systems, ensuring that HANA EE license consumption KPIs in the SAM4U tool are also populated:

Pic. 03. Creation of Consolidation

That’s it! You’ve done it yourself 😊 Now SAM4U KPIs are filled, and you can enjoy the detailed analysis of your license consumption. In our example, we noticed we're using more licenses than we're entitled to 😉

Pic. 04. Users’ Licenses Consumption Analysis

Install and enjoy the SAM4U tool, or get it hosted by us to keep your costs under control.

P.S. Check out our entire blog series about the SAM4U tool at this link: SAM4U as a Service.

entry.title: Use SAM4U Tool to Optimize Consolidated User Classification and Avoid License Compliance Gaps
entry.content:
Haven't heard about the SAM4U tool from SAP that helps manage and optimize your license adoption and consumption? This tool provides clarity and eliminates the need for costly professional services you might be hiring now. The only numbers you can trust when discussing licenses are those from SAP. SAM4U, recently released to SAP customers, answers all your license consumption questions. See it in action on our website: Skybuffer!

Streamline Your License Management with SAM4U

Today, we'll explore a simple use case of analyzing consolidated data from your SAP backend system to provide insights into the number of users classified automatically during system metering or audit. These users are often assigned to the most expensive professional metric of your price list, generating high costs for the company. Manual user classification for on-premise systems is the key to proper SAP license entitlement consumption.

Preparation Steps:

  1. Install SAM4U Extractor: Install the SAM4U extractor in all SAP backend systems that are part of the consolidation (measurement plan).
  2. Extract and Upload Data: Extract data from the SAP backend and upload it to the SAM4U tool.

Once these steps are completed, you can examine the Classified/Dialog Users KPI:

 Pic.01. SAM4U Users Analytics - Classified/Dialog Users KPI

The Classified/Dialog Users KPI shows you:

  • Licensed Users: The number of “seats” of different types purchased from SAP to cover SAP user IDs in your landscape. If the Licensed number is smaller than the Dialog number, as in our example, you have a license compliance gap. You can either clean your SAP backend systems (discussed in future blog posts) or purchase additional SAP licenses.
  • Dialog Users: The consolidated number of SAP user IDs across all systems in your SAP landscape.
  • Classified Users: The consolidated number of SAP user IDs manually classified in SAP backend systems. This means your SAP Basis team has set user classification via the SU01 transaction during user ID creation or mass classified users via the USMM2 transaction, for example.

Important: If the number of Classified users is smaller than Dialog users, it indicates that some users (the delta between Dialog and Classified 😊) are automatically classified and assigned the maximum license type, potentially leading to extra license costs. Manual classification of these users can result in significant savings!

By clicking on the Classified bar, you can view the list of all user IDs that are automatically classified, re-classify them, and improve your SAP license consumption.

Pic.02. SAM4U Users Analytics – A List of Automatically Classified Users

Simple and valuable, isn’t it? If you'd like to see SAM4U in action, the demo instance is available on our website: Skybuffer, where you can also get your private SAM4U instance for productive use.

P.S. Check out our entire blog series about the SAM4U tool here: SAM4U as a Service.

entry.title: To RISE or Not To RISE
entry.content:
This is a key decision facing most organisations that run their SAP estate in an on-premise environment or natively on a hyperscaler and are thinking about the future.

It is not an easy decision to make and requires deep analysis relating to their current operating model, the resource and skill mix available and SAP’s future roadmap and strategy. Organizations also need to identify the expected operational, commercial and process benefits to justify the move to RISE and change their status quo.

Having been associated with RISE with SAP since its inception, I have seen how SAP’s offering has matured over the years. SAP is making significant investments into the operational and delivery capabilities of RISE. SAP’s strategy has also evolved to fully support this cloud model. It is my firm belief that NOW is the right time for customers to decide and move to RISE with SAP. Here are some of the reasons.

 

Maturity

RISE with SAP was launched by SAP’s CEO, Christian Klein in January 2021. Since then, the offering has matured significantly. The commercialisation of a large proportion of SAP’s  previously on-premise solution offerings has occurred to allow them to now be offered in the cloud model.

There has been significant investment in tools, automation and people so that the delivery of the cloud service is world class. The ‘Enterprise Cloud Services’ or ECS organisation is the delivery arm of SAP for the RISE offering where these investments have occurred.

ECS supports the operational delivery & support of the RISE offering and is separate to the implementation services which implements S/4HANA. ECS is purely involved in the operational, day-to-day, business-as-usual (BAU) maintenance and management of customer’s SAP landscape under RISE.

 

New Board Area - Customer Services & Delivery

At the start of 2024, a new board area was established called, ‘Customer Services & Delivery’ which brings together all the resources involved in the architecture and delivery of RISE and associated services. This board area now has architecture and pre-sales teams involved in architecting our customer’s cloud solutions (Cloud architects), the services teams that implement the S/4HANA capabilities (Services teams) and the teams involved in the actual day-to-day operational delivery of service for RISE customers (ECS team).

This was a strategic decision and is extremely important as it reflects the internal transformation within SAP as it expands from an engineering, product development, and a sales organisation, into a cloud service provider ensuring customer success through adoption and delivery of cloud services and focuses on achieving successful customer outcomes.

Our customers will gain from RISE with SAP by utilising SAP’s expertise and experience in managing S/4HANA customers globally and benefiting from SAP’s focus on customer adoption and outcome driven approach.

You can read about these here.

 

Incentives

Since RISE with SAP launched, SAP has been listening to customer feedback and has released the RISE with SAP Migration and Modernisation program in Jan 2024. The news article is here.

Through this program SAP recognises the complexity of the transformation required and offers to actively support customers on their journey to the cloud and to help make the transition easier.

The program offers customers a Migration Methodology to provide guidance across their migration journey, providing roadmaps for innovations, and more significantly, it offers commercial incentives for customers moving to RISE.

This incentive is called the RISE Transformation Incentive and is a limited time fund through till the end of 2024 that SAP has created to support customers moving to RISE. Through this program, SAP customers can leverage this fund and receive incentives in the form of credits as a means of offsetting some of the costs involved in their transformation journeys.

This incentive will be offered to current SAP S/4HANA and SAP ECC on-premise customers moving their SAP landscape to RISE and they will stand to gain credits as a percentage of the amount of their RISE with SAP subscription. These credits can be utilized to offset either the on-premise maintenance costs, implementation services costs or the cost of the RISE subscription fee itself.

To date I have engaged with several customers that have benefited from this incentive. It is my view that this incentive fund represents a significant opportunity for customers to initiate their move to RISE in 2024.  

 

Partner Ecosystem

When RISE was first launched, our partner ecosystem was quite apprehensive. However, over time, our partners have also understood that RISE with SAP is at the core of the SAP Cloud Strategy and have now jointly worked with SAP to tailor their own offerings based on the RISE implementation methodologies.

Several of SAP’s partners have also become RISE customers including Accenture, IBM, Wipro, HCL, Microsoft, which further goes to show the strength of the offering and the trust that partners, most of whom have their own SAP practices, bestow upon SAP in managing their SAP landscapes for them.

 

So, if you are sitting on the fence and trying to decide whether the time is right to move to RISE, I would say that 2024 is definitely the year you should make the decision and take the plunge. I can safely say that you will get the kind of support from SAP that you would have never seen before!

entry.title: Evaluate Consolidated Manual Classification of SAP Users to Discover SAP License Potential Savings
entry.content:
Do you manage multiple SAP systems and have a measurement plan for SAP audits but haven't proactively analyzed whether your manual classification of SAP users aligns with your SAP license entitlement? With the SAM4U solution from SAP, this task becomes effortless, helping you maintain your SAP landscape within entitled SAP license boundaries.

How it works?

Install the SAM4U extractor from SAP in your SAP backend systems, gather data with almost one click, and enjoy consolidated analysis done by the SAM4U Tool.

Where to get SAM4U extractor?

The SAM4U Extractor is deployed into the customer's backend systems to extract data. Its functionality has been adapted (downported) for compatibility with release 7.31 and is distributed via SAP Note:
3292019 - SAM4U Data Extractor - SAP for Me

Which SAM4U KPI shows consolidated results of manual classification of SAP users?

Once you have uploaded data to the SAM4U tool and created a consolidation according to your SAP measurement plan, as shown in the example below:

 Pic.01. SAM4U Consolidation

You can access all SAM4U KPIs that are automatically filled with data based on the extracted information from SAP backends. A demo instance of the SAM4U Tool is always available on our website: Skybuffer

On the Dashboard, as well as in the Users Analytics section, you can find the “Classified Users” KPI, which displays the number of classified users for each type.

 Pic.02. SAM4U Tool – Classified Users KPI

By clicking on the chart, you can drill down to the list of users.

 Pic.03. SAM4U Tool – List of Users Assigned to a Certain Metric

In our example, you can see that 402 SAP users are classified as “SAP Bus. Suite Professional,” which is the most expensive type of license. Checking the License Consumption KPI in the SAM4U tool, we see that the number of “SAP Bus. Suite Professional” licenses purchased is less than the number of SAP users manually classified as professionals.

Pic.04. SAM4U Tool – License Consumption

Optimizing Manual Classification of SAP Users

Considering the spare licenses of other SAP user types, several options exist for optimizing the manual classification of SAP users to comply with SAP license entitlement:

  • Evaluate Manual Classifications: Use the SAM4U KPI “Classified/Dialog Users” from user analytics to ensure all users are manually classified. Automatically classified users are typically assigned the most expensive license type, so reviewing and reclassifying these users is essential.

  • Identify Dormant Users: Check the SAM4U users analytics for dormant users who might be locked and invalidated.

  • Analyze User Roles and Usage: Use the SAM4U tool to identify users whose roles and active usage do not justify their current classification, allowing for potential reclassification.

SAM4U Tool brings clarity and helps you reclassify SAP users effectively, ensuring proper SAP license consumption and unlocking potential cost savings.

Get familiar with the SAM4U tool available on our website: Skybuffer and keep reading our blogs.

P.S. Check out our entire blog series about the SAM4U tool here: SAM4U as a Service.

entry.title: Track SAP Usage Consistently and Efficiently with the SAM4U Tool
entry.content:
If you are new to our blogs, you’ll be excited to learn about the SAP SAM4U Tool, a solution that allows you to track SAP usage consistently and efficiently.

Pic. 01. SAM4U Tool – Dashboard

Discover the official SAM4U Tool on our website: Skybuffer.

Why Analyze SAP Usage?

Every SAP customer aims to maintain license compliance. Consistent SAP usage monitoring helps identify and address license compliance gaps, either by redistributing existing SAP licenses or optimizing and ordering only the necessary license entitlements to close the gap.

What Can You Analyze with the SAM4U Tool?

With the SAM4U Tool, you can analyze consolidated license consumption data from various SAP backend systems and compare it with current licensing information using one of the following methods:

  • SAP users’ authorizations
  • SAP active usage of the systems
  • SAP manual users’ classification

How to Optimize SAP License Consumption

The SAM4U Tool offers a set of KPIs that help optimize SAP license consumption. For example, you can perform a dormant users analysis to see the list of locked users in all consolidated SAP systems or the list of users inactive in all systems for over 180 days.

Pic. 02. SAM4U Tool – Users Locked in All Systems

Pic. 03. SAM4U Tool – Users Inactive in All Systems for Over 180 Days

Additionally, you can analyze authorization-based consolidated SAP users’ classification:

Pic. 04. SAM4U Tool – SAP Users Classification based on Authorizations

Or active usage-based consolidated SAP users’ classification:

Pic. 05. SAM4U Tool – SAP Users Classification based on Active Usage

Both approaches provide valuable information for better SAP license redistribution, helping you maintain compliance.

Get Started with SAM4U

Now that you are familiar with the SAM4U Tool, explore it further on our website Skybuffer and stay tuned to our blogs for more insights.

Take control of your SAP usage and ensure license compliance with the SAM4U Tool today!

entry.title: Phase-Based Maintenance Process With Lean Services in SAP S4 HANA Asset Management.
entry.content:
In this blog I would like to outline the comprehensive phase-based maintenance process that supports in the maintenance of technical objects, and also will touch base lean services framework, which is fully embedded in the phase based maintenance process.

What is Phase-Based Maintenance Process?

The phase-based maintenance process supports the order types "Reactive Maintenance" and "Proactive Maintenance" , also determines the process phases that the maintenance order runs through. It has been designed to be used within the functional scope of the Reactive Maintenance (4HH) and Proactive Maintenance (4HI) scope items. This process differs from the maintenance process that has been delivered with respect to scope items BH1, BH2, and BJ2.

If we create specific type of maintenance requests and maintenance orders, these maintenance requests and orders run through nine phases, Viz: Initiation, Screening, Planning, Approval, Preparation, Scheduling, Execution, Post Execution and Completion.

When we execute maintenance activities, at certain point, we must need lean services to complete our maintenance activities and have to depend on external service providers to incur lean services.

So, What is Lean Services?

Lean services are intended for the procurement of small and highly specialized services that are carried out by external service providers as part of an internal or external maintenance operation. Lean services can be both planned or un-planned. Planned lean services are services for which the exact value is know at the time of procurement. Un-Planned lean services are services for which the exact value is not known at the time of procurement, so SAP has provided an option to input value limit that must not be exceeded and however, the processing of lean services purchase requisition that has been created for maintenance operation follows the standard sourcing and procurement process flow. Standard Item categories to have lean services are, 

1. Non-Stock Item (N)  for planned lean services, where Material Type should be SERV and Product type group should be "2".

2. Enhanced Limit (E) for unplanned lean services with a value limit.

My Business Use-Case:

My organization has got a heavy rotary equipment, i.e. Rotary Kiln and we all know that kiln is more dynamic when compared to other equipment as it can be easily influenced by a lot of process parameters.

For last few weeks we have been observing that, there has been a huge swift in delta "T" , i.e. temperature difference between kiln shell and tyre, also has had excessive skew of the rollers which caused abnormal floating of the kiln and due to which thrust roller has got excessive load, hence, my maintenance team has decided to have a hot kiln alignment to understand the Kiln shell profile, shell ovality and axial movement. So, I will be processing this entire exercise using "Reactive Maintenance" process in phase-model with lean services, i.e. Hot kiln alignment work.

Disclaimer: This blog does not cover the aspects of "Work Permit" integration with maintenance order.

I am using below technical object structure for demonstration purpose.

To begin with, let us understand Phases and Sub Phases of Phase-Model at notification, order header and operational level.

Also, let us have a look at high level configuration settings to be done to have phase based maintenance process.

Activate Scope ID 4HH for DE - Ensure order type YA01 is set for "Phase Model" and  "Approval Required".

Activate enhanced procurement mode for YA01- Reactive Maintenance Order type to enable lean services procurement through reactive maintenance order.

Mini Note: 

  1. When a maintenance order type is enabled for lean services procurement, a new "Service" tab page is displayed in the operation details of the maintenance order. 
  2. When maintenance order type is activated for " Enhanced Procurement Mode", maintenance orders of that order type support lean services and do not support externally processed operations that are defined as a service by control key PM03.

Configure overall status profile for YA01 order type with status and map phases & subphases to overall status profile - Status profile with overall statuses record the progress of maintenance activities when active phase model for order type is set.

Assign status profile PMSP1 to order type YA01,

Fact Sheet:  In the phase-model, notification context allows us to create a maintenance request that do not have to follow all the nine steps. Based on notification processing context, the maintenance request can have exception and skip few steps of the maintenance process.

  • If I create a Maintenance Request with no processing context, system processes nine phases of maintenance process - When order is in "Approval Phase" and orders are submitted for approval, and system uses flexible workflow process. 
  • If I create a Maintenance Request with processing context as "Emergency Work", system skips the screening and assigns the emergency order and puts the workorder in planning phase.
  • If I create a Maintenance Request with processing context as " Minor Work", system accepts respective maintenance request as soon as it is submitted. This processing context is used to record malfunction data for maintenance issues that can be fixed immediately and do not need a maintenance order.

For my business use case, I am going to create a maintenance request with no processing context , hence, I will be walking through the nine phases of the phase model.

During "Initiation" phase, I have created a maintenance request for Kiln-01, using "Create Maintenance Request App", and Input the required information to process hot kiln alignment work. 

and maintenance request is prioritized based on combination of consequence categories, consequences and likelihoods. 

once the maintenance request is submitted, the maintenance request can be viewed under "Submitted" tab.

The submitted maintenance request moves to "Screening" phase. As a supervisor, I screen the maintenance request and accept it.

Accepted maintenance request moves to the "Planning" phase. As a maintenance planner, now I can create and plan order. Once the order gets created, it is in phase Planning , subphase is In Planning(Order).

Once order is created, system updates the order number in field "Associated With:"

As a maintenance planner, I have to plan maintenance activities, i.e lean services, that are to be performed by an external party . As the order type YA01 is configured for lean services, I am going to use control key PM01 not the PM03.

I have created lean services master data as,

and also created main work center with HR assignment to carry out maintenance operations. The start time and end time of the work center is crucial if we align the lean services with the main work center.

Now, let us maintain the required operations for the maintenance order 4000230 and add respective lean services. I have got a planned lean service item, 587 - Hot Kiln Alignment Service and un-planned lean service item with enhanced limit, i.e. resurfacing of supporting rollers.

when I assigned the lean services to operation 0010, system automatically  aligned the start date and time of lean services with earliest start date and time of the operation.

Now, I have selected the option "align with work center" for the assigned lean services, so the system distributed the service duration over several days as per my main work center capacity utilization.

post assignment of lean services and operation man hours, system has updated the planned cost,

As I have configured the manual workflow for maintenance order type YA01, the maintenance order 4000230 goes through the workflow steps for approval. ( If the workflow is configured for automatic approval, then the order is approved immediately). As it is planned for manual approval, as a planner, I have submitted the maintenance order 4000230 for approval.

The submitted maintenance order 4000230 moves to my Inbox for approval. Maintenance orders are approved through a flexible workflow process in the "Manage Workflow For Maintenance Orders" app.

I have created a blog, which outlines manage workflows for maintenance orders and is available on SCN and can be accessed using below link, 

Manage Workflows for Maintenance order. 

When maintenance order is submitted for approval, the phase and sub-phase of the maintenance order becomes "Approval" and "Submitted for Approval (Order)" respectively.

Orders that are ready for approval are available in "My Inbox" app.

I have approved the maintenance order 4000230 with a decision note.

while approving, approver can even check the planned cost of the maintenance order & workflow log as well.

If the maintenance order is approved, then the sub-phase of the maintenance order is "Approved (Order)". If the maintenance order is rejected, then the sub-phase of the maintenance order is "Rejected (Order)".

Approved maintenance order 4000230 can be released and its phase moves to "Preparation".

Despite having the maintenance order 4000230 subphase is "Approved (Order)", system does not pass the maintenance order to "Preparation" phase and does not even allow to release the maintenance order - It has got  the "Preparation" phase and "In Preparation (Order)" sub-phase and phase control key  H001 value as "Block" as my business use case demands below configuration settings for phase control key activation.

Now I deactivated and proceeded to release maintenance order.

now the maintenance order 4000230 has moved to "Preparation" phase,

As a maintenance planner, I have got to structure my maintenance backlogs. To do so, I have created a operational planning bucket , which includes maintenance scope, i.e. Planner Group as MEC & Main Work Center as MECH along with time duration as 7 Days, so that system automatically groups my maintenance orders having same planner group and main work center and associates them with my planning bucket.

I have created planning bucket for "Calcination Mechanical" work orders,

Now system has associated maintenance order 4000230 based on given scope to my planning bucket "CAL_MECH", and I can now monitor readiness of the maintenance order.

Maintenance Backlog app provides a aggregated information about several maintenance orders and allows us to navigate to other relevant applications, including manage components and services app.

A Purchase Order 45000000095 has been created for the hot kiln alignment lean services,

I am now confident that all the required resources will be available as per my planned dates, so I have submitted maintenance order for scheduling.

Post submission for scheduling, system has updated maintenance order 4000230 status to ORSC (Ready To Schedule) and all the operations passed on to the "Scheduling" phase - Maintenance Order 4000230 header and operations phase is "Scheduling" & Subphase is Ready To Schedule.

Now, I have dispatched maintenance order operations.

Post dispatch, maintenance order 4000230 has moved to phase "Execution" & Subphase "Ready For Execution".

Post dispatch, maintenance technician receives all the work order operations having stage MAIN & PRE  that need to be executed. Using "Perform Maintenance Jobs" app, maintenance technician can confirm the work order operations.

based on the actual spent hours, I have recorded the time stamp details.

Now, actual work gets updated and operation 0010 system status is CNF -  Confirmed.

Though it is not a breakdown, we have got to perform root cause analysis for the malfunction. As per the KILN failure data profile, I have performed root cause analysis.

we can assign multiple causes for object part damage, however, we can only choose one cause as a main "root cause" for the malfunction.

and I have even documented the activities that were carried out for the malfunction rectification.

and as part of the hot kiln alignment work, I have also recorded measurement documents for the kiln measuring points to ensure that the kiln condition is up to the mark. As per the assignment of measuring points to the technical object, system displays all the measuring points, so that it would be handy for the maintenance team to capture the measurement readings as part of the work order closure.

Now, as part of lean services procurement, I have created a service entry sheet against P.O: 4500000095 and same was approved.

I have created a blog, which outlines flexible workflow for service entry sheet - lean services and is available on SCN and can be accessed using below link.

 All About Planning, Adding & Approving Lean Services in SAP S/4HANA Asset Management. 

Now, the MAIN operation has got confirmed and also the service entry sheet got approved.

Let us have a look at the actual cost of the maintenance order.

After completion of the work, the final confirmation check box is selected, the operation sub-phase becomes "Work Finished".

After confirming all the operations, I have got to change the maintenance order 4000230 status to " Main Work Completed". 

As part of "Post Execution" phase, I am proceeding to perform technical completion,

Now, the maintenance order has moved to phase  "Completion" & subphase "Technically Complete". During this phase, maintenance planner or financial controller reviews the maintenance order cost and settles the order.

Now, finance controller settles the actual cost to respective cost center of the technical object.

and now finally, the maintenance order status changes to "Complete". There by the maintenance order is set to "Closed (Order)" subphase.

Now let us have a glimpse of maintenance order cost analysis report and document flow,

This brings to the end of phase based maintenance process and 😊 I hope this blog proves useful to maintenance team and I really look forward to seeing maintenance team using phase based maintenance process and lean services to enhance their reliability maintenance aspects.

 

--

Yours Sincerely,

Pardhasaradhi Reddy.C

entry.title: SAP for Me for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition
entry.content:
(Eric Sutanto is Cloud Architect & Advisor in Cloud Architecture & Engineering, SAP Enterprise Cloud Services. He helps customer in the journey for RISE with SAP Private Cloud Edition (PCE) by conducting Technical Architecture Session, various workshop in terms of security, R&R, and working closely with Key Stakeholders, Sales/Account Team, SAP Services or chosen SI/Partner to ensure smooth journey during presales, onboarding and S2D handover as their trusted advisor.
Eric Sutanto is PMP, CCSK, 9xMulti-Cloud Certified in AWS, Azure, GCP in Solution Architect, Network, Security domain with more than 22 years of SAP experiences.)

Introduction

SAP for Me is the Customer Portal for the Intelligent Enterprise. The launch of SAP for Me marks the beginning of a new customer experience.

SAP for Me is for all SAP customers, new, existing, and even on premise as replacement for SAP Support Launchpad Portal. SAP for Me is your digital companion to easily interact with SAP and get immediate guidance to the best solution for you. SAP for Me aggregates important alerts, metrics, and insights about your SAP product portfolio with a single access point. This blog will give simple navigation guide on SAP for Me Private Cloud Workspace, Early Watch Workspace, Hot News and Service Level Performance Category Overview for new and existing RISE SAP Private Cloud Edition customer. (Note: valid S-ID or Universal ID for customer is required to login and access the apps).

1. Private Cloud Workspace

Useful landing page for RISE SAP Private Cloud Edition customer to view the following:

  • Upcoming scheduled events
  • My Service Requests
  • Top Issues
  • Backup status
  • System capacity
  • Security Compliance
  • Quick Links to other applications e.g., availability, EWA, Security Notes, etc.
CategoryService and Support Dashboard in Private Cloud Workspace
Navigation Guide
  1. Go to “Private Cloud Workspace”
  2. You will see “Service Requests” Dashboard
  3. You will find a list overview of the Service Request accessible with a simple click.
CategoryTop Issues
Navigation Guide
  1. Go to “Private Cloud Workspace”
  2. Go to “Top Issues” for visibility on priority issue for your attention.
CategoryBackup Status
Navigation Guide
  1. Go to “Private Cloud Workspace”
  2. Go to “Backup Status” for quick overall status for your backup.

 

CategorySystem Capacity
Navigation Guide
  1. Go to “Private Cloud Workspace”
  2. Go to “System Capacity”
  3. Click on the graph to find more details about the inspected capacity.

 

CategorySecurity Compliance
Navigation Guide
  1. Go to “Private Cloud Workspace”
  2. Go to “Security Compliance on the bottom for overall status on security patch.

3. Go to “Systems with Missing Patches” to find the status and request patch

CategoryQuick Links
Navigation Guide
  1. Go to “Private Cloud Workspace”
  2. Go to “Quicklinks” to other applications e.g., availability, EWA, Security Notes,etc.

2. Service Level Performance Category Overview

There are 5 key Apps under Service Level Performance Category Overview as described on this section with URL/Link for easy navigation.

  • Availability and SLA Overview
  • Cases
  • System Landscape Overview
  • DB Performance
  • Service Request Overview
CategoryAvailability and SLA Overview
Navigation Guide
  1. Go to “System & Provisioning”
  2. Go to “Availability”
  3. Go to section “Events”, for list overview with all the outages and latest status.
  4. Use the filters for categorized overview of the outages

5. Tick “SLA Relevant”

6. Expand the Event ID to see the system details.

7. Go to section “Availability Reporting”, and use filters for categorized overview

CategoryCases
Navigation Guide
  1. Go to “Services & Support”
  2. Go to “Cases”
  3. Go to “Manage cases” to get an overview of the open incidents
  4. Click on each case to find more details

CategorySystem Landscape Overview
Navigation Guide
  1. Go to “System & Provisioning”
  2. Go to “Systems”
  3. Check your Cloud and Private Cloud Systems, click for further details
CategoryDB Performance
Navigation Guide
  1. Go to “Private Cloud Workspace”
  2. Go to “Quick Links” - “DB Performance” to find details on DB performance
CategoryService Request Overview
Navigation Guide
  1. Go to “Services & Support”
  2. Go to area “Service Request”
  3. Go to “New Service Request” to create a new request

3. Early Watch Workspace

The Client Delivery Manager (CDM) discuss together with customer during the monthly periodic review using Early Watch Workspace to address any red alert items and important key issues.

4. SAP HotNews

SAP HotNews are priority 1 (very high) SAP Notes written to help resolve and prevent issues in SAP systems. Customer and partners can access the SAP HotNews apps in SAP for Me portal.

CategorySAP HotNews
Navigation Guide
  1. Go to “SAP for Me”
  2. Go to “SAP HotNews”
  3. You can click on the box (or boxes) next to the article(s) you want to highlight.
  4. Use the “Confirm” or “Not Relevant” button to mark the HotNews Notes

Conclusion

In conclusion, the Private Cloud Workspace, EWA Workspace, SAP Hot News, and periodic Service Performance Review Report provides an important role in assuring the service(s) delivered, are aligned with the requirements of the contract and ultimately the Business /Customer community. Customers, with their respective SAP RISE PCE Support contact review the service performance report and get the latest out of their system’s performance. Plan together the Action Plan, which best suits the customer’s case.

Acknowledgement

The author would like to express deep appreciation for Aditya Khorana (Head of Client Delivery Management, SEA) and Varat Sastryoungkul (Client Delivery Manager, SEA) for the support in reviewing the content and providing valuable feedback.

Disclaimer

© 2024 SAP SE or an SAP affiliate company. All rights reserved. See Legal Notice on www.sap.com/legal-notice for use terms, disclaimers, disclosures, or restrictions related to SAP Materials for general audiences.

entry.title: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Upgrade Faster - More use cases for Launchpad Content Aggregator
entry.content:
Following on from my previous blog post SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – 3 simple use cases for Launchpad Content Aggregator, here are a few more you might find useful, particularly if you are upgrading to a higher SAP S/4HANA release.

This blog post is relevant for customers running SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition.  For example customers running RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition tailored option.

Hint: This blog post is also relevant for customers running the SAP S/4HANA product on other infrastructure such as on-premise server or hyperscaler.

IMPORTANT: These screenshots include some new features added in SAP S/4HANA 2023 and SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01, including new features added as explained in SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA - Composite Roles in launchpad content and layout tools. 

In this blog post you will find:

  • Hints on using the Launchpad Content Aggregator
  • How to find the SAP Fiori apps and classic UIs assigned to a role
  • Creating a consolidated list of your assigned SAP Fiori apps and classic UIs for the SAP Fiori Upgrade Impact Analysis
  • Finding deprecated and obsolete apps and their successors
  • Finding apps with errors and warnings
  • Finding tile differences on upgrade between a source and target SAP S/4HANA release
  • Comparing SAP Business Roles & SAP Business Catalogs to custom business roles & custom business catalogs
  • Finding apps with OData V4 services

Hint: Classic UIs includes other content that is typically launched from the SAP Fiori launchpad - such as SAP GUI Transactions, ABAP Web Dynpro applications, and Web Client UIs.

Hint: The screenshots are taken from a SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 Fully Activated trial system. Refer to SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Trial system options for Private Cloud and On-Premise

 

Hints on using the Launchpad Content Aggregator

These are a few hints to help you make the most of the Launchpad Content Aggregator.

You access the launchpad content aggregator using transaction /UI2/FLPCA.  This transaction can be run from SAP GUI or launched from the SAP Fiori launchpad. You can include it in a custom business role if you wish.

The Launchpad Content Aggregator contains a lot of columns.  You will usually want to adjust the displayed columns depending on your use case.

Select Change Layout to adjust the layout. You will find these in the

You can also use:

  • Save Layout to save your layout
  • Choose Layout to select a saved layout
  • Manage Layouts to edit/delete your layouts you have saved

Within the Change Layout dialog there are 4 tabs: Displayed Columns, Sort Order, Filter, and Display.

In the Displayed Columns tab

  • You can select all displayed columns or select individual columns
  • You can use the move arrows between Displayed Columns and Column Set (i.e. all remaining columns) to adjust which columns are displayed. You can even use your usual keyboard shift key to select several columns and move them together.
  • You can use the up/down/top/bottom arrow heads to move the displayed columns into the order you want

See an example below of the Change Layout dialog on the Displayed Columns tab.

In the Sort Order tab of the Change Layout dialog, you can sort the data by columns. You can choose whether a column uses an ascending or descending sort.

In the example below the data is sorted by:

  • Role Ascending
  • Title/Subtitle/Information Ascending

In the Filter tab of the Change Layout dialog, you can restrict what is shown by choosing the columns and then using Define Values to enter the values you want to see. An example of the filter tab of the Change Layout dialog is shown below.

You can Save your layout for future use, use Save As... in footer of the Change Layout dialog, or Save Layout... from the main menu tab.

In the example below the layout is saved with name AppsByRole and description Apps Per Role. Note that the / before the role name is required.

You can download your result data.

In this example you see how to download the results to spreadsheet using the Export menu button to choose your export file type. 

App Types in the Launchpad Content Aggregator include:

  • UI5 = SAP Fiori app
  • WDA = Web Dynpro ABAP application
  • WCF = Web Client UI
  • GUI = SAP GUI Transaction (i.e. WebGUI)
  • URL = URL Tile/Target Mapping
  • URLT = URL Template-based Tile/Target Mapping
  • <blank> = Tile only

 

How to find the SAP Fiori apps and classic UIs assigned to a role

You can run this at any time in your source or target release of your SAP S/4HANA system.

Procedure:

Open the launchpad content aggregator.

Select all your custom roles by adjusting the Role Filter and pressing Execute.

 

In the result list, set as Displayed Columns:

  • Composite Role Name
  • Single Role Name 
  • Title/Subtitle/Information
  • Application Type
  • Application Resource
  • SAP Fiori ID
  • Transaction
  • Device Type Desktop
  • Device Type Tablet
  • Device Type Phone

The results list shows you the apps assigned by your roles.

You can optionally download these results. For example, you could use these results to work out:

  • The number of apps per role
  • Number of Fiori apps vs classic UIs per role (using the App Type)
  • Confirm which apps of the role will be visible on different device types
  • Check what keywords can be used to find apps in Search on Apps

 

Creating a consolidated list of your assigned SAP Fiori apps and classic UIs for the SAP Fiori Upgrade Impact Analysis

IMPORTANT: Your source SAP S/4HANA release must be on SAP S/4HANA 1909 SPS04 or SAP S/4HANA 2020 FPS01 or higher to access the Launchpad Content Aggregator.

Procedure:

In your source SAP S/4HANA release, execute the Launchpad Content Aggregator for your custom business roles.

Follow the same approach as for creating apps by role.   Download the list of apps.

Remove all rows with App Type = blank (tile only) or URL or URLT.

Create a consolidated list of apps for the SAP Fiori Upgrade Impact Analysis, that is create a single column that contains:

  • For SAP Fiori apps ( App Type = UI5 ) the SAP Fiori ID
  • For SAP GUI transactions ( App Type = GUI ) the Transaction
  • For Web Dynpro applications ( App Type = WDA ) the Application Resource
  • For Web Client UI applications ( App Type = WCF ) the Application Resource

Convert the list to a comma separated file (suffix .CSV) which you upload to the SAP Fiori Upgrade Impact Analysis.  Refer to SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Upgrade Impact Analysis

Finding deprecated and obsolete apps and their direct successors

This is useful to run in your target SAP S/4HANA release to find what content needs to be replaced by successors.  If a direct successor is listed, it means you can use the quick fix feature to replace them.  Refer to SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Upgrade Faster – Managing app lifecycle impacts on users

You will need to replace references to obsolete content with their successors prior to regenerating your custom business roles and other custom security roles. Obsolete content is no longer supported by SAP, and usually no longer exists, so it will cause errors if not removed.

You should add successors for deprecated apps prior to regenerating your custom business roles and other custom security roles.  Deprecated content is still supported, but will be made obsolete in a future release.  You should aim to transition to successors as soon as possible. To minimize upgrade effort, you should remove all references to deprecated content prior to your next upgrade.

IMPORTANT: You can add successors and remove obsolete/deprecated content using the Launchpad Content Manager Client-Specific – transaction /UI2/FLPCM_CUST.  The column Transaction Status identifies obsolete and deprecated content in your Roles, Catalogs, Apps/UIs. The feature Show/Add Successors in the Catalog tab can be used to quickly add direct successors of deprecated and obsolete content.

Procedure:

In the Launchpad Content Aggregator, select your custom buisness roles. Select the option to Display Successor Transactions, i.e. direct successors. Then Execute.

Change the layout to include the following Displayed Columns:

  • Composite Role Name
  • Single Role Name
  • Single Role Transaction Status
  • Catalog ID
  • Catalog Transaction Status
  • Title/Subtitle/Information
  • Application Type
  • Application Resources
  • SAP Fiori ID
  • Transaction
  • Transaction Status
  • Successor Transaction, i.e. direct successor

 Hint: If you are using composite roles, also include Composite Role Transaction Status.  

 

 Example results below: You can see the Transaction Status column shows if the app is Deprecated or Obsolete, and the Successor Transaction column shows whether there is a direct successor. 

To condense the list to only deprecated and obsolete content, you can use the Filter tab to filter on column Transaction Status. Using Define Values, select both the Deprecated and Obsolete values.

Hint: You should Save your layout for future use.

 

You can get a consolidated list and even download it for offline reference.

Finding apps with errors and warnings

Common errors you might encounter during a SAP S/4HANA release upgrade are:

  • Reference Lost
  • Reference Lost to Backend Catalog
  • Configuration Error

The error “Reference Lost” indicates the referenced content no longer exists. It may have been deleted or moved. Checking for a duplicate navigation intent – i.e. Semantic Object and Action combination – can be useful in resolving moved content.

The error “Reference Lost to Backend Catalog” suggests you may need to replicate the backend catalog to resolve the error.

Configuration errors are rare. They indicate incomplete or misaligned configuration. So you will need to examine the tile and target mapping in the original technical catalog to resolve the problem.  

For further advice on how to resolve these errors, refer to: SAP Note 3226560 - SAP Fiori Launchpad: Preventing and Mitigating Lost References after an Upgrade

Procedure:

Execute the Launchpad Content Aggregator in your target SAP S/4HANA release for your custom business roles.

Change the layout to display the following Displayed Columns:

  • Status Text
  • Composite Role Name
  • Single Role Name 
  • Catalog ID
  • Navigation Intent
  • Title/Subtitle/Information
  • Original Target Mapping Catalog ID
  • Application Type
  • Application Resource
  • SAP Fiori ID
  • Transaction
  • Service Activation Status Text

Filter on column Status Text to filter on selected errors and warnings. 

 

Example results below showing Lost Reference errors and Configuration erros. 

 

Finding tile differences on upgrade between a source and target SAP S/4HANA release

You will run the launchpad content aggregator in both your source release and your target release.

It is helpful to include the Device Type as which device types are supported can change between releases. For example, due to improved capabilities to support smaller devices, or due to changes in the app that make it no longer suitable for use on smaller devices.

It is helpful to include the Catalog Title as the title is shown in the App Finder and Home navigation button, which can change how users find a particular app.

It can be useful to include Tile Keywords which can be used to find apps using the Search on Apps in the SAP Fiori launchpad shell bar, as this can help users find the correct tile even if the tile texts have changed.

Hint: There can also be changes in the icons used on tiles. However, since the icons are considered to be decorative these changes are not usually significant.

IMPORTANT: Prior to SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 you can only select the single role in the displayed columns.

Procedure:

Start the launchpad content aggregator.

Select your custom business roles and execute.

Change the layout to display the following Displayed Columns:

  • Single Role
  • Catalog ID
  • Catalog Title
  • Title/Subtitle/Information
  • Application Type
  • Application Resource
  • SAP Fiori ID
  • Transaction
  • Tile Keywords
  • Device Type Desktop
  • Device Type Tablet
  • Device Type Phone

Sort the list by:

  • Single Role Description
  • Catalog Title
  • Application Type
  • Application Resource
  • SAP Fiori ID
  • Transaction

 

 

Example results: You can see the tile texts in Title/Subtitle/Information. Particularly watch out for "deprecated" or "obsolete: added to tile texts.

Download both lists and compare the source and target lists.

 

Comparing SAP Business Roles & SAP Business Catalogs to custom business roles & custom business catalogs

When you have refined your own custom business role or business catalog from a SAP Business Role or SAP Business Catalog, you may want to compare them to check for differences.

In the launchpad content aggregator, when selecting the roles use the multi-selection feature to select the roles you want to compare.

Below you can see the multi-selection feature at the end of the Role Filter row.

 

Select the roles you want to compare.

Change the Displayed Columns to those you  want to compare

  • Single Role Name
  • Catalog ID
  • Title/Subtitle/Information
  • Application Type
  • Application Resource
  • SAP Fiori ID
  • Transaction  

 

To restrict the comparison to a catalog, use the Filter dialog to filter on column Catalog ID. Use Define Values to select the Catalog ID(s) you want to compare.  You can use the multiple selection feature again for this.  

Hint: Remember to include both the SAP business catalog id and the custom catalog id.

 

Change the Sort Order to make it easier to spot differences. Remember that not every entry in a catalog may have a tile – some may be reuse apps or other dependencies that are only held as target mappings.

For example, sort on:

  • Application Type
  • Application Resource
  • SAP Fiori ID
  • Transaction
  • Catalog ID

 

Example results below show it is easy to compare the catalogs and spot differences.  You can make this even easier by downloading the results to spreadsheet, then removing duplicates rows with the same combination of:

  • Application Type
  • Application Resource
  • SAP Fiori ID
  • Transaction

 

 

Finding apps using ODataV4 services

This can be useful as the configuration, activation and troubleshooting for apps using ODataV4 services is somewhat different to those apps using ODataV2 services.

Hint: For SAP Fiori apps, focus on OData services. The need for ICF services has been reduced in SAP S/4HANA 2023 as explained in SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – OData V2 maintenance innovations with S/4HANA 2023.

You run this procedure in your target SAP S/4HANA release.

Procedure:

Start the launchpad content aggregator.

Select your custom business roles then choose the parameter Display OData V2 Services and Display OData V4 Services.

Displayed Columns are:

  • Composite Role Name
  • Single Role Name
  • Catalog ID
  • SAP Fiori ID
  • OData V4 Service Group Name
  • OData V4 Service Name
  • OData V4 Service Version
  • OData V4 Service Status
  • OData V4 Additional Information 

 

To minimize the results, as only SAP Fiori apps have ODataV4 services you can filter on:

  • Application Type = UI5
  • ODataV4 Service Name 0* to Z*

Example results: You can see which roles/catalogs/apps are assigned to which OData V4 service group.

 

Becoming a SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA guru

You’ll find much more on our  SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA topic page

Other helpful links:

  • See all questions and answers about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA 
  • Follow SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA for more blogs and updates 
  • Ask a Question about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG and Customer Care team.

entry.title: Dynamic HTTP or Odata Adapter – SAP Cloud Integration – CI/CPI
entry.content:
In this blog, I aim to simplify the use of dynamic resources/querying in Odata or HTTP.

To make it clearer for you, let's imagine the following scenario:

You need to perform a GET request in Odata on the S4hana Public Cloud, S4hana Private Cloud, or S4hana On-Premise, and you want to use the query options in a very dynamic and simple way.

Examples of queries:

 

https://myXXXXXX-api.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/A_BusinessPartner('17300002')

https://myXXXXXX-api.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/A_BusinessPartner?$filter=BusinessPartnerGrouping eq 'BP03'

 

Many times, we want to call SAP Cloud Integration - CPI from a SAP or non-SAP system, passing these parameters. How can we make CPI accept this without giving an HTTP 404 error?

This is what I will teach you step-by-step in this blog, using the Odata Adapter and HTTP Adapter.

Let's get to work!

 

Spoiler alert: the end of our journey

Step 1º:

The first thing we will do is put an asterisk '*' in the Address field of our HTTP adapter.

Example: /testURL*

With this in the CPI monitor, we will have a URL like the one below:

 

https://9265fbe0trial.it-cpitrial05-rt.cfapps.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com/http/testURL*

 

Step 2º:

Configure the Router component.

Here we are using a Header to check whether to execute oData or HTTP; this step is entirely optional. I did it this way for didactic purposes, to show that it is possible to use both the odata adapter and the HTTP adapter. In this blog, you will see the difference between the two.

Step 3º:

Create a local Integration Process.

Step 4º:

Inside the local Integration Process, we will create a router. In this Router, we will check a standard CPI Header. If CPI is called with the URL below, it fills the CamelHttpPath.

 

https://myXXXXXX-api.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/A_BusinessPartner('17300002')

 

In this case, we are searching for a record by its key field. So we will use the READ route.

Step 5º:

We will add a Request Reply in the iflow.

Step 6º:

We will add the Odata adapter.

If you don't have an S4hana environment to use, you can use the Sandbox from SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

Link to API Business Partner: https://api.sap.com/api/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/overview

If you have doubts about using the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, here are links to explanatory videos:

- Link in Portuguese (Brazil) - Video 1

- Link in Portuguese(Brazil) - Video 2

- Link in English - Video 1

- Link in English - Video 2

In the Address field, you either put the address of your S4hana environment or the SAP Sandbox.

https://myXXXXXX-api.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER

https://sandbox.api.sap.com/s4hanacloud/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/A_BusinessPartner

You should create your credentials in CPI, whether they are Basic, Oauth2, or even API Key (the latter should be passed in the header as Authorization).

In the Processing tab, select Operation Detail as READ(GET).

In the Resource Path, add the entity plus the header that contains the key field data.

Example: A_BusinessPartner${header.CamelHttpPath}

Step 7º:

Now we will practically repeat the step to create another Request Reply and odata adapter.

The only additional point is that we will add a Groovy script to check if the CamelHttpQuery header is empty. If it is empty, we will create a property called isEmptyHeader and set it to ‘true’; otherwise, it will be ‘false’.

 

 

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;
import java.util.HashMap;
    
def Message processData(Message message) {    
 //Headers
 def headers = message.getHeaders();
 def isEmptyHeader = headers.get("CamelHttpQuery") == null || 
 headers.get("CamelHttpQuery").toString().isEmpty()

 message.setProperty("isHeaderEmpty", isEmptyHeader)

return message;
}

 

 

the Router we check this property.

If it follows the empty route, then we will create this header with a blank value using the content modifier.

Notice that in the Connection tab, it is identical to the previous one.

In the Processing tab, add the standard CPI header CamelHttpQuery.

This header is filled automatically when we send a URL like the one below, which is done when we pass a FILTER in the URL.

https://myXXXXXX-api.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/A_BusinessPartner?$filter=BusinessPartnerGrouping eq 'BP03'

The oData part is now done. Now let’s see how it works with HTTP.

 

Step 8º:

Now we will create the Local Integration Process to use with the HTTP adapter.

Here is a detail: it is necessary to copy a header to a property because we need to delete the value of this header.

In this Content Modifier, we are moving a header to a property.

In this Content Modifier, the CamelHttpPath header is being deleted. This step is mandatory; if not done, it will cause an error in the HTTP call. I recommend you test without deleting it to understand why we remove it.

Step 9º:

Now we will add a Request Reply and use the HTTP adapter.

In the HTTP adapter, we will configure it as follows. It will be the address of your S4hana endpoint or Sandbox, as we saw earlier, plus the property we created in the Content Modifier.

https://myXXXXXX-api.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/A_BusinessPartner${property.CamelHttpPath}

Change the Method to GET. 

Add the credentials to access the environment. If using an API Key, add the Authorization header.

Our development is now complete.

Now let's test using POSTMAN.

Test 1º:

We will call the Odata adapter without passing any parameters or filters. In this case, we want the Odata to return all the records in the database.

Test 2º:

Calling by passing the key field in the URL. In this case, it will follow the READ route.

Test 3º:

In this test, the Filter is being tested in the URL.

With this, the iFlow went through the QUERY route.

Test 4º:

In this test, we are testing the HTTP adapter without sending any filter or key. We expect the return to be all BP records from S4hana.

Test 5º:

In this test, we will test the HTTP adapter by passing a Key in the URL to return only one record. The adapter should perform a "READ".

Important: In this test, the CamelHttpPath header is filled, which is why we delete it in the Content Modifier. The HTTP adapter uses it automatically if it exists. Test to form your own conclusions!

Test 6º:

We will test the HTTP adapter by passing a filter in the URL.
